channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: We Built Email for AI Agents
item.description: We've spent the past two years building Atomic Mail, a privacy-focused email provider with end-to-end encryption. Along the way it became obvious that AI agents are going to need a way to talk to people and to each other, the same way humans do over email. So we pointed our email infrastructure at...
item.dc:creator: Atomic Mail

item.title: LINTelligent — AI-Integrated service for code linting and reviewing with MCP server
item.description: Introduction This is a post I need to share with you my thought process, decisions that I took in designing the service, and share new things that I learned. I hope anyone can benefit from my words or inspire anyone with my ideas and what I learned so we both can grow. GitHub Repository:...
item.dc:creator: Mazen Aly

item.title: Atomic settlement is Sybil-blind by design - and that is why a counterparty directory sits above it
item.description: Last week a reader left a comment on one of our posts that was sharper than most product feedback we get. The point, paraphrased: a hash-time-locked contract is Sybil-blind. It cannot distinguish a real counterparty from a fake one, or one identity from ten thousand spun up by the same actor. The...
item.dc:creator: Baris Sozen

item.title: Day 10/30: Accurate Citations
item.description: I still remember the day our research agent, designed to summarize complex scientific papers, started producing impressive-looking citations that seemed too good to be true. And they were. Upon further investigation, we found that the agent was hallucinating sources, fabricating references to...
item.dc:creator: Kasi Yaswanth

item.title: MCP Server to generate custom QR codes directly in Cursor and Claude
item.description: If you use Cursor, Claude Desktop, or Cline as your daily driver, you know how annoying it is to leave your editor just to generate simple project assets. Last week, I was testing print layouts for a physical marketing project that needed scannable codes. I was tired of opening browser tabs,...
item.dc:creator: Evgenii Zinner

item.title: MCP Enrichment: Giving Your AI Agent a Verified Data Layer It Can Call Over MCP
item.description: MCP Enrichment: Giving Your AI Agent a Verified Data Layer It Can Call Over MCP A great rep once knew every account. Now your agents do. The problem is the data underneath them. Most AI-agent go-to-market stacks today are built on a quiet lie: the enrichment layer looks functional until an agent...
item.dc:creator: Stuart McLeod

item.title: Multi-agent mesh is not a product — unless agents can discover it
item.description: Daily LuisCore syndication · 2026-07-20 · angle only-we-do-this "Agent mesh" became a buzzword. LuisCore's Chorus Field is a public coordination layer with MCP-native tools, scoped write keys, and a discovery manifest agents fetch without human onboarding. Discovery first Agents bootstrap via...
item.dc:creator: luisprimecore

item.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP), Explained
item.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP), Explained If you've built an AI assistant or agent that needs to talk to external tools — a research paper search, a database, a web search API — you've probably run into the same problem: every tool needs its own custom integration code. Two or three tools,...
item.dc:creator: Anuj Tyagi

item.title: During an incident, ChatGPT should gather evidence, not become root
item.description: During an incident, the expensive part is rarely writing one SQL query. It is preserving the same service, environment, tenant, deployment, and time window while responders move between errors, changes, health events, and runbooks. A ChatGPT database connection can help maintain that context. It...
item.dc:creator: Mads Hansen

item.title: Your MCP connection pool is an admission-control system
item.description: An MCP database server can be read-only, well scoped, and still take production down. The failure path is familiar: several agents call tools at once each tool borrows a database connection a few queries run longer than expected the pool fills callers queue retries turn a burst into sustained...
item.dc:creator: Mads Hansen

item.title: Gateway Talents in Solon AI: OpenAPI, Tool, and MCP Surfaces That Scale Without Context Blow-Up
item.description: When an agent can see 80 tools at once, the model does not get smarter. It gets noisier. Token bills climb, tool choice drifts, and a simple “check order status” prompt suddenly carries half of your company’s OpenAPI surface. Solon AI (v4.0.3) answers that with the Gateway Talent trio in...
item.dc:creator: Solon Framework

item.title: Agent App vs MCP Server: The Honest Structural Difference
item.description: If you're building tools for AI agents, you've likely come across two approaches: agent apps (installable local capability services) and MCP servers (remote tools exposed via the Model Context Protocol). They look similar on the surface — both let agents do things beyond text completion — but they...
item.dc:creator: Philip Stayetski

item.title: The night four MCP servers started acting like one system
item.description: Last weekend I watched an agent workflow do something I had been waiting to see outside a demo. Hermes needed to hand a detailed review to another coding agent, start a fairly heavy Codex job, avoid overloading the local server, and leave enough evidence for me to check what happened afterwards. It...
item.dc:creator: Stephen Phillips

item.title: Beyond text: Returning images and interactive apps from MCP servers
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming a richer foundation for agent experiences. Though most servers return plain text from their tool calls, MCP servers can also return binary results and provide interactive apps in clients that support those features, like VS Code. In this post, I'll use...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis
